settings.title: Settings
settings.appearance.language.help: Interface language. Automatic follows your system or browser language.
settings.preview.intro: Colors for how the 3D viewport renders the simulated stock once cutting preview lands. Stored now so the eventual renderer picks them up automatically. The 3D preview style (wireframe / solid / both) lives under View above.
settings.preview.cell_size.title: Voxel resolution for the sim heightmap. Below 0.05 mm explodes RAM; above ~2 mm loses tab + sliver detail. Cap is 5 mm to keep the sim sane.
settings.performance.caps_hint: Max simulation cells caps the WASM heightmap grid (and so the simulation accuracy). Max render triangles caps the 3D-sim preview's mesh size: the renderer automatically drops to a coarser LOD level when zoomed out or when simulation cells exceed the budget. Simulation accuracy is preserved; only the rendered mesh degrades. Stepped voxel mesh ≈ 6 triangles per cell.
settings.performance.exact_rewind.hint: The 3D heightfield is a forward-only carve simulator: cells can only get deeper. When on (default), every backstep resets the sim and replays the carve from t = 0 to the new playhead so the heightfield exactly tracks the playhead's position in time. Replay cost scales with the number of segments replayed; on programs with tens of thousands of segments scrubbing can stutter. When off, backstep is a no-op for the sim — the heightfield retains the deepest cut at each XY from the last time the playhead reached that segment. Useful for fast scrubbing on huge programs when you don't need time-accurate rewind, BUT note: after every Generate the playhead jumps to 1.0 so warnings surface, which means the off setting lands the heightfield at end-of-program until you re-scrub forward.
settings.safety.block_critical: Block G-code generation on critical sim warnings
settings.safety.block_work_area: Block G-code export on out-of-work-area moves
settings.safety.block_work_area.title: When on, exporting/saving G-code is blocked if the last Generate found moves outside the machine work area (a soft-limit fault or gantry crash on the real machine). Off by default because the work-area envelope is often a placeholder — turn it on once you've set your machine's real travel. Generate/preview stay available so you can see and fix the violation.
settings.safety.auto_run_sim: Auto-run sim on every project save
settings.safety.auto_regenerate.title: Debounces ~1.5 s after the last edit, then runs Generate G-code. Off by default so power users on big projects keep manual control.
settings.safety.hint: "Critical" warnings are collisions and rapids cutting through material. With the block enabled, fixing them — or disabling the safety check — is required before downloading G-code.
settings.sources.auto_reload: Auto-reload imported DXF / SVG when changed externally
settings.sources.hint: Desktop only. Watches the source file backing the current import and re-runs it when the CAD app saves a new version (one undoable step). Disable to get a "Reload?" toast instead. Network and OneDrive-synced paths can drop events silently — manually re-import if a save doesn't show up within a few seconds.
settings.snap.midpoint.help: Snap to the midpoint of each segment.
settings.snap.grid.help: Snap to integer multiples of the grid step below.
settings.snap.grid_step.title: Grid step (mm) when 'Grid' snap is on. Cursor latches to integer multiples of this offset from the project origin.
settings.snap.hint: Object snap on the 2D canvas. Endpoint / midpoint / intersection / center latch the cursor to existing geometry features; Grid latches to abstract grid spots independent of the drawing.
machine.title: Machine
machine.storage_note: Lengths are stored in mm regardless of the Unit selector below — the Unit only switches the G20/G21 word in emitted gcode.
machine.profile.label: Profile
machine.profile.pick.title: Machine profile — a named machine setup plus its own tool library, saved on this computer (not in the project). Switching profiles swaps both into the project in one undoable step. While a profile is selected, edits to the machine or the tool library are saved back into it.
machine.profile.dirty_switch.title: You have unsaved machine edits — OK or Cancel them first, then switch profiles.
machine.profile.save_as.title: Save the machine settings shown below together with the project's current tool library as a new profile, and link this project to it.
machine.profile.duplicate.title: Copy this profile (machine + tools) under a new name — for variants of the same machine.
machine.profile.delete.title: Remove this profile from this computer. The project keeps its current machine settings and tools — nothing in the project is deleted.
machine.profile.missing_note.title: This project references a machine profile that doesn't exist on this computer (it was saved elsewhere). The project loaded with its own embedded machine + tools — use 'Save as profile' to recreate the profile here.
machine.name: Name
machine.name.label_title: Free-text identifier for this machine setup. Shown in the dialog header + persisted into .ivac-machine.json save files.
machine.name.input_title: Machine name. Left empty, the proposal (mode + work area) is used when saving this machine.
machine.unit.title: Output units in the emitted G-code (G20 inch / G21 mm). Internal storage is always mm — this only affects the post.
machine.mode.title: Primary mode — drives the G-code emitter. Mill: subtractive CNC, full Z control. Laser: M3/M5 power, ignores Z. Drag: vinyl cutter / drag knife, emits HPGL. Plasma: torch with a pierce/cut-height entry sequence. Adjust 'Capabilities' below if the machine can do more than this primary mode.
machine.capabilities.legend_title: Which op kinds the machine can run. The op-picker hides kinds the machine doesn't support — e.g. a laser-only machine never shows Drill. Defaults to just the primary Mode above if left empty.
machine.capabilities.primary_title: The primary mode (selected under Mode above) is always available — pick a different Mode to remove it.
machine.fast_move_z.title: Safe Z height for rapids between cuts. Spindle rapids to this height before XY moves so the tool clears clamps and stock.
machine.work_area.legend_title: Machine work envelope in mm — the stock auto-defaults to this size when no drawing is loaded, and (future) sim checks use it as the soft-limit reference.
machine.emit_comments.title: Include (parenthesized) comments in the G-code — section markers, op names, tool numbers. Disable for controllers that reject comments.
machine.emit_arcs.title: Fit curved polylines into native G2/G3 arc moves where possible. Yields smaller, smoother G-code. Disable if your controller has buggy arc handling.
machine.arc_tol.title: How far the fitted arc may deviate from the original polyline. Smaller = tighter, more arcs split. Typical values 0.005-0.05 mm.
machine.toolchange.title: How the post handles a tool change between ops with different tools. ATC: emit T<n> M6, the changer swaps automatically. Manual (M6 prompt): grblHAL / FluidNC — emit M6, the controller parks and prompts the operator. Manual (M0 pause): portable M0 program pause for stock GRBL / Marlin, which reject M6. Ignore: emit no tool-change handling (you manage swaps yourself).
machine.optional_stop.title: Emit M1 (optional stop) instead of M0 (mandatory stop) at every program pause — the Pause op and the manual tool-change halt. M1 is honored only when the controller's optional-stop switch is ON, so a vetted program can run unattended (switch off skips the pauses) yet still stop on demand. Off = mandatory M0.
machine.laser_dynamic.title: GRBL only: emit M4 (dynamic power) instead of M3 for laser cuts and raster engraving. The controller ramps the S power with the actual feed rate, so corners and edges — where the head slows — don't over-burn, and rapids force S0 automatically. Strongly preferred for laser engraving. Requires GRBL laser mode ($32=1). Off = portable M3.
machine.plot_mode.title: Plot-mode Z: collapse every cut to a single pass at the op's cut depth and skip the multi-step descent / ramp / helix machinery. Z values in G-code are restricted to fast_move_z (pen up) and cut depth (pen down). Right setting for laser / plasma / pen plotters / 3D-printer extrusion and drag-knife controllers.
machine.dialect.title: Output dialect for this machine's controller. LinuxCNC: standard RS-274 G-code. GRBL: hobby-CNC subset with manual tool-change prompts. HPGL: vinyl-cutter / plotter language (drag-knife mode).
machine.decimal_sep.title: Some EU-locale Siemens / Heidenhain controllers require X1,5 instead of X1.5. Default is the period.
machine.line_num.title: Prefix every emitted line with N10, N20, N30, … Required by some FANUC / vintage controllers; useful operator reference even on modern ones. Empty / 0 disables numbering.
machine.hpgl_note: Drag mode emits HPGL plotter commands, not G-code. The post-processor profile (templates, axes, etc.) is ignored — HPGL has no analogue for these tokens.
machine.profile_preset.title: Pick a built-in profile or write your own templates below. Built-in profiles fill the templates with sensible defaults for that controller; you can still edit them. 'None' uses ivac's hard-coded defaults.
machine.rapid.title: G0 rapid feed rate. Used by the simulator for time estimates; most controllers ignore the F-word on G0 and use their own internal max.
machine.toolchange_time.title: Wall-clock seconds spent on an M6 tool change. Used by the simulator for total runtime estimates.
machine.jerk_enable.title: Use S-curve (jerk-limited) acceleration in the simulator. Better matches modern controllers (LinuxCNC trajectory planner, MachineKit). Off = simple trapezoidal velocity profile.
machine.spindle_min.title: Lower spindle-RPM clamp (M3 S<rpm>). Tool / op RPMs below this clamp UP to the min and emit a 'spindle_speed_clamped_below_min' warning. Empty = no floor (default).
machine.spindle_max.title: Upper spindle-RPM clamp. Tool / op RPMs above this clamp DOWN to the max and emit a 'spindle_speed_clamped_above_max' warning. Empty = no ceiling (default).
machine.max_feed.title: Upper feed clamp. Cutting / plunge feeds above this clamp DOWN to the max and emit a 'feed_clamped_above_max' warning, so an out-of-range feed can't reach the controller. Empty = no ceiling (default).
machine.spindle_start_dwell.title: Spindle-start dwell inserted into the M6 tool-change envelope after M3 S<rpm>. Lets the spindle reach commanded RPM before the next cut. Stacks with the per-tool ToolEntry.pause. Empty = 0.5 s default.
machine.spindle_stop_dwell.title: Spindle-stop dwell inserted between M5 and the actual T<n> M6. Gives the spindle time to spin down before the chuck is touched. Most VFD spindles want 0.5–1 s; high-inertia big-iron may want 1–2 s. Set to 0 to skip. Empty = 0.5 s default.
machine.park_home.title: When on, the program_end footer emits G53 G0 X0 Y0 — retract to machine home after the safe-Z lift, before spindle-off and M30. When off, falls back to G0 X0 Y0 in the current WCS (work zero) and the optional Park XY below applies.
machine.park_xy.help: Optional — empty = retract to work zero (0, 0). Set both fields to route the head to a specific load / tool-station point after the safe-Z lift.
machine.save.title: Save this machine config to a .ivac-machine.json file.
machine.load.title: Replace the active machine config with the contents of a .ivac-machine.json file.
ops.picker.select_first: Select one or more objects in the canvas first.
ops.help.profile: Cuts along the contour of selected geometry. Tool stays inside, outside, or on the path.
ops.help.pocket: Removes material inside a closed boundary. Choose Cascade/Zigzag/Spiral/Trochoidal strategy.
ops.help.pocket_outside: Pocket the area BETWEEN a frame and the selection. Useful for raised text/graphics where the surrounding area is removed. Requires at least one selected object.
ops.help.drill: Drills holes at point geometry or small closed circles. Choose simple / peck / chip-break cycle.
ops.help.engrave: Tool-on engraving along the source path. No offset.
ops.help.drag_knife: Drag-knife cuts with trail-compensation arcs at corners.
ops.help.t_slot: Undercut pass along the source path at the floor depth with a T-slot cutter. Pre-cut a stem slot ≥ the neck width first; enter laterally (the wide head cannot plunge through the narrow stem).
ops.help.dovetail: Undercut pass along the source path at the floor depth with a form / dovetail cutter — its angled flanks carve the walls. Rough a straight channel ≥ the profile’s narrowest width to depth first, then drop the bit in.
ops.help.vcarve: Variable-depth medial-axis carving with a V-bit. Tip dips deepest where the region is widest.
ops.help.chamfer: Chamfering pass with a V-bit. Set the chamfer width and the depth is derived from the bit angle.
ops.help.thread: Single-point thread milling inside a circular bore (internal) or around a stud (external). Requires a closed-circle selection.
ops.help.pause: Optional-stop: emits M0 with an operator message at this slot. The machine halts so the operator can change tools manually, inspect the cut, or flip the stock. Press Cycle Start to resume.
ops.help.homing: Sends the machine to its predefined home position with G28, optionally followed by a rapid retract to safe Z. No tool, no cut — program scaffolding.
ops.help.probe: Touch-probe move (G38.2) along the chosen axis. Used at program start to zero the WCS Z against the stock top, between ops to re-establish a reference, or as a sanity check.
ops.help.cycle_marker: Comment-only marker. Emits a wrapped label at this slot — pendants index by program line so the operator can jump here.
ops.help.gcode_include: Splices an external G-code file into the program at this slot. Supports {x} / {y} / {z} / {f} / {s} / {safe_z} variable substitution. The sim does not model the included block — inspect canned cycles by hand.
ops.help.relief_mill: 3D relief surfacing from a grayscale image with a ball-nose cutter. Brightness becomes height; load an image, set the depth range and scallop. Rough the bulk first with a flat endmill.
ops.help.raster_engrave: Laser raster engraving from a grayscale image. Burns row-by-row, modulating laser power per pixel (dark = hotter). Load an image, set resolution and a power curve (linear / threshold / dither).
dialog.text.title: Add Text
dialog.text.text.title: Text to render. Newlines split into multiple lines (MTEXT).
dialog.text.font.bundled.title: Use a font bundled with ivacam. Bundled fonts are filled-outline (good for V-carve / pocket / drag-knife — not single-line engraving).
dialog.text.font.custom.title: Load any TTF/OTF from disk. Single-line / Hershey fonts are auto-detected and required for the Engraving style.
dialog.text.size.title: Cap height of the text in millimeters.
dialog.text.width.title: Horizontal stretch (50–200 %). 100 % = the font's natural width.
dialog.text.pos_x.title: X-position of the text origin (left baseline) in stock coordinates.
dialog.text.pos_y.title: Y-position of the text origin (left baseline) in stock coordinates.
dialog.text.engraving_mismatch: This font is filled-outline. Engraving style needs a single-line / Hershey font.
dialog.text.tool.title: Tool to use for this text op. The list is filtered to tools matching the style's required kind.
dialog.text.depth.title: Final Z depth for the text op. Negative values cut into the stock — e.g. -0.5 mm for a typical engraving.
dialog.text.error.no_engraving_font: Pick a single-line / Hershey TTF via "Custom font" — no engraving font is bundled yet.
dialog.open_drawing.title: Open drawing
dialog.open_drawing.body: Open this drawing as a new project, or add it to the current layers?
dialog.unsaved.title: Unsaved changes
dialog.unsaved.body: Your project has unsaved changes. Save before you {action}?
dialog.export_stl.no_stock: No simulated stock to export — run Generate first.
dialog.quit.title: Quit ivaCAM?
dialog.quit.unsaved_body: You have unsaved changes. Save before you quit?
dialog.quit.confirm_body: Are you sure you want to quit?
genbar.error.block_critical: {count} critical warning(s) (collisions / unsafe cuts) — fix or disable the safety check in Settings before downloading
genbar.error.block_work_area: {count} move(s) leave the machine work area — fix the path / work-offset, widen the work area, or disable the work-area gate in Settings
genbar.error.no_operations: Add at least one operation to generate G-code.
genbar.chip.idle: Warnings: not run yet — Generate first
genbar.sim_idle_hint: Sim verification runs after Generate. Catches rapid moves through stock, fixture collisions, and cutter holder collisions before you cut.
genbar.generate.title: Run the CAM pipeline and produce a toolpath. Reads the current ops, tools, stock, and machine — output is cached so unchanged ops re-emit instantly.
genbar.generate.title_stale: The visible toolpath is stale — the project has changed since the last Generate. Click to refresh.
genbar.download.title: Save the generated toolpath to disk in the selected dialect's file extension.
genbar.download.stl_title: Export the simulated (carved) stock as a binary STL mesh.
genbar.source_stale.title: Source file changed on disk: {path}. Reload to pick up the changes; Ignore to keep the current view.
genbar.panel.title: Warnings ({count})
genbar.panel.empty: No warnings — sim and pipeline are clean.
genbar.row.sim_title: Surfaced by the simulator after G-code generation.
genbar.row.goto_title: Move the 3D playhead to this warning
genbar.row.pipeline_title: Surfaced by the CAM pipeline during G-code generation.
genbar.row.apply_fix_title: Snap the WCS origin to the geometry bbox's bottom-left corner — the canonical CNC zeroing convention.
toast.queue.title: More errors queued — dismiss to see the next
layers.caret.collapse.title: Collapse layers (return to previous panel)
layers.caret.expand.title: Expand layers
layers.toggle_all.title: Show / hide every layer
layers.file_stats.title: Segments · layers
layers.title: Layers
layers.add.title: Add a layer — open a drawing or add text geometry
layers.add.open_file.title: Open a DXF or SVG file
layers.add.text.title: Add editable text geometry
layers.import_remove.title: Remove this drawing from the project
layers.xform.head.title: Layout convenience: translate / rotate / scale / mirror this drawing. Pivot for rotate / scale / mirror is the drawing's original bbox center.
layers.xform.label: File transform
layers.xform.reset.title: Reset to identity (no transform)
layers.xform.tx.title: Move this drawing by this many mm in X. Positive = right.
layers.xform.ty.title: Move this drawing by this many mm in Y. Positive = up.
layers.xform.rotate.title: Rotate around the drawing's original bbox center. Positive = counter-clockwise.
layers.xform.scale.title: Uniform scale around the bbox center. 1 = no scale, 2 = twice as big, 0.5 = half size.
layers.xform.mirror_x.title: Mirror across the horizontal axis through the bbox center (flip top↔bottom). Negates arc bulges so curvature stays valid.
layers.xform.mirror_y.title: Mirror across the vertical axis through the bbox center (flip left↔right). Negates arc bulges.
layers.row.toggle.title: Active = included in pipeline + visible on canvas. Uncheck to deactivate.
layers.row.delete.title: Delete this layer (drops every segment on it)
layers.empty.title: No drawing yet
layers.empty.sub: Import a DXF or SVG to see its layers here.
canvas.firstrun.tap_select: Tap an object to select it
canvas.firstrun.click_select: Click an object to select it
canvas.ctx.empty_hint: Select objects to add an operation, or a text layer to reposition it.
canvas.ctx.set_text_origin.title: Move the selected text layer's left-baseline origin to the right-clicked spot.
canvas.fit_view.title: Fit view to scene (F)
canvas.add_to_selection.title: Add to selection (tap multiple objects)
canvas.help.title: Keyboard & mouse shortcuts (?)
tools.title: Tool library
tools.file.save.inventory.title: Export the shop inventory (as shown, including unapplied edits) to a .ivac-toolset.json file.
tools.file.save.project.title: Save the current tool library to a .ivac-toolset.json file.
tools.file.load_replace.inventory.title: Replace the shop inventory with the contents of a .ivac-toolset.json file (takes effect on Apply).
tools.file.load_replace.project.title: Replace the current tools with the contents of a .ivac-toolset.json file.
tools.file.load_add.title: Add tools from a .ivac-toolset.json file. Tools whose name already exists are skipped.
tools.search.title: Filter by name, comment, or kind.
tools.filter.runs_on.title: Show only tools that can run on this machine type.
tools.col.id.title: Sort by tool number
tools.col.name: Name
tools.col.name.title: Sort by name
tools.col.kind.title: Sort by kind
tools.col.diameter.title: Sort by diameter
tools.col.tip_angle.title: Full apex angle for V-bits / engravers — drives V-Carve depth.
tools.col.flutes.title: Sort by flute count
tools.col.speed.title: Sort by spindle speed
tools.col.feed.title: Sort by feed rate
tools.col.plunge.title: Sort by plunge rate
tools.col.dflt_step.title: Default Z step (depth-per-pass) for operations using this tool. Negative number, mm. Empty = no default — every operation must set its own.
tools.row.expand.collapse.title: Collapse holder section
tools.row.expand.expand.title: Expand holder section
tools.row.name.title: Tool name. Leave empty (or keep the proposal) and it follows the settings automatically — type your own to pin it.
tools.row.diameter.invalid.title: Tool ⌀ must be greater than 0 mm — zero / negative values produce no toolpath.
tools.row.tip_diameter.invalid.title: Tip ⌀ must be ≥ 0 mm — the V-Carve depth math (z = -(r - tip_r) / tan(angle / 2)) silently clamps negative values to 0, hiding the typo.
tools.row.tip_angle.title: Full apex angle of the V cone in degrees. Drives V-Carve depth (z = -(r - tip_r) / tan(angle / 2)) and Chamfer width. Common values: 30°, 45°, 60°, 90°.
tools.row.speed.invalid.title: Spindle speed must be ≥ 1 RPM — zero / negative values emit no S word and the controller may refuse.
tools.row.feed.invalid.title: Feed rate must be ≥ 1 mm/min — zero / negative values emit no F word and the controller stalls.
tools.row.feed.drill.title: For drill, this is the plunge feed.
tools.row.plunge.invalid.title: Plunge rate must be ≥ 1 mm/min — zero / negative values cause the controller to refuse the move.
tools.row.dflt_step.invalid.title: Default step must be NEGATIVE (mm down per pass) — values ≥ 0 are ignored and treated as unset.
tools.row.dflt_step.title: Operations using this tool inherit this when they don't specify their own. Negative number, mm.
tools.row.delete.disabled: At least one tool must remain
tools.row.delete.title: Delete tool
tools.holder.runs_on.title: Machine modes this tool kind can physically run on — a machine stocks only compatible tools.
tools.holder.flute_length.title: Length of the cutting flutes from the tip up. Sets the height above the tip where the shank starts. Empty = treat the entire tool as cutting (no holder check).
tools.holder.overall_length.title: Overall / usable tool length (mm), tip to where the shank enters the collet. Display + 3D-preview only — it does NOT change the G-code (reach is driven by flute length + stickout + holder). Sets the preview tool's total height. Empty = diameter-derived heuristic.
tools.holder.shank_diameter.title: Shank diameter above the cutting flutes. Empty = same as the cutting diameter (parallel-shank bit).
tools.holder.stickout.title: Free shank length between the top of the cutting flutes and the bottom of the holder/collet (mm). Models reach-extension tooling where the collet doesn't grip right above the flutes. Empty / 0 = legacy behavior (collet sits directly on flutes).
tools.holder.preset.title: Apply common ER-style holder presets — fills in flute length, shank, and holder fields with conservative ballpark values.
tools.holder.label: Holder
tools.pass_overrides.title: Per-tool overrides for the wall-defining (finish) pass of a Pocket op and for Drill ops. Empty = inherit the main speed/feed/plunge values.
tools.drill.peck.title: Default peck step for Peck / ChipBreak drill cycles whose op leaves peck_step_mm at 0. Empty = the op must specify its own.
tools.drill.xy_overlap.title: Per-tool default XY overlap for pocket / cascade ops (dr5). Empty = fall through to the global 0.5. Clamped to 0.05–0.95 at generate time.
tools.comment.placeholder: Notes about this tool — material, vendor, sharpening date, etc. Appears as the tooltip on the tool dropdown in op properties.
tools.comment.title: Free-text description. Doesn't affect any pipeline output.
tools.z_shift.title: Per-tool Z origin offset. For machines without auto tool-length probing. Pre-measure each tool's tip Z relative to a reference and record the delta. Positive = sticks out further; negative = shorter. A G92 Z<shift> line is emitted at program start and after each tool change. Empty / 0 = no shift.
tools.spindle_warmup.title: Dwell (seconds) emitted as G4 P<n> after every M3 / M4 so the spindle reaches commanded RPM before the cut starts. Critical on machines without spindle-at-speed feedback. Empty = default (1 s).
tools.spindle_dir.title: z1y0: spindle direction the post commands when this tool is selected. CW (M3) is the default; CCW (M4) is for left-hand cutters / reverse-thread / mirror-helix tooling.
tools.whirl.title: Whirling (orbital milling) overlay. When enabled with an Extra width > 0, every cut move with this tool is subdivided and the cutter orbits in a helical spiral around the toolpath — keeping radial engagement bounded at every point, for chip-thinning on hard material. This is milling whirling, not lathe thread-whirling; to cut threads use a Thread operation with a thread-mill tool. Set Extra width to the orbit diameter, Stride to the path distance per revolution, Z-wobble to a small dip for chip clearance.
tools.whirl.extra_width.title: Diameter (mm) by which the whirling orbit widens the cut. Empty / 0 ⇒ overlay disabled (whirling is a no-op).
tools.whirl.stride.title: Path distance per full spiral revolution. Empty = half the spiral radius (one-revolution overlap).
tools.whirl.z_wobble.title: Z ripple amplitude. The cutter dips up to 2·osc below the cut plane between revolutions, improving chip evacuation. Empty / 0 ⇒ flat (no Z motion from the overlay).
tools.drag.title: Drag-knife fields. The drag offset drives the pivot-arc compensation at corners.
tools.drag.offset.title: Distance from the spindle axis to the cutting tip. The post emits a pivot arc at each corner so the blade trails through cleanly. 0 ⇒ no compensation (true tangent knife).
tools.drag.self_align.title: Corners with a tangent change below this angle skip the explicit swivel arc — real drag knives self-align below ~30° via the trailing offset. Set to 0 to force a swivel at every corner (legacy behaviour). Empty = 30° default.
tools.compression.title: Compression / up-down cutter: down-cut flutes on the upper part, up-cut on the lower, meeting at the transition height — clean edges on both faces of sheet stock.
tools.compression.transition.title: Height above the tip where the down-cut flutes flip to up-cut. Set to your stock thickness so the flip lands at the bottom face. Display + preview only in v1 — it does NOT change the cut cross-section. Empty = the preview assumes the flute midpoint.
tools.thread.title: Single-point thread mill: cuts threads by helical interpolation. The tip ∠ in the main row is the thread flank angle (60° metric / 55° Whitworth).
tools.thread.pitch.title: Thread pitch (mm) — the axial advance per orbit. e.g. 1.0 for M6×1, 1.5 for M10×1.5. Drives the helical Z-advance of the Thread op.
tools.bullnose.title: Bull-nose endmill: corner radius rounds the cutter's bottom edge.
tools.bullnose.corner_radius.title: Radius of the rounded corner where the flat bottom meets the side. Set to 0 (or empty) for a square endmill; set to half the diameter for a ball-nose.
tools.wear.title: Wear compensation. Toolpaths cut at the nominal diameter minus this offset — the bit's TRUE cutting diameter after wear or a regrind. The nominal diameter above stays what's printed on the bit.
tools.wear.offset.title: Difference between the bit's nominal and measured cutting diameter (positive = worn smaller). Empty / 0 = cut at the nominal diameter. Run Calibrate to measure it.
tools.wear.calibrate.title: Measure the bit's true cutting diameter with a slot test cut and store the wear offset.
tools.wear.stale.title: This measurement is more than 90 days old — bits keep wearing; re-run the calibration.
tools.laser.title: Laser-only fields. Honored when this tool fires the cut.
tools.laser.pierce_time.title: Seconds the beam dwells at the entry point with laser ON before the cut begins. Critical for piercing thick acrylic / wood — without it the first millimeter is gouged. Emitted as a G4 P<sec> between rapid-to-entry and plunge.
tools.laser.lead_in.title: Per-tool lead-in distance (mm) the laser head travels before reaching the cut path, to reduce edge entry burn. Used as the lead-in length for laser ops that don't override leads themselves.
tools.laser.kerf.title: Laser kerf width (mm) — the heightmap-side spot radius the sim carves at. Empty / 0 = the legacy 0.15 mm default in the Rust sim. Set to your measured kerf on the actual stock for accurate heightmap previews.
tools.plasma.title: Plasma torch entry sequence. The torch pierces at the pierce height, dwells the pierce delay, then drops to the cut height for the cut.
tools.plasma.pierce_height.title: Height above the stock where the arc is established before dropping to the cut height. Too low and the torch sticks to the slag; too high and the arc drops out. Typical 3–5 mm for 1–3 mm steel. Empty = 3.8 mm default.
tools.plasma.cut_height.title: Height above the stock the torch drops to for the actual cut (below the pierce height). Typical 1.5–2.5 mm for thin steel. Empty = 1.5 mm default.
tools.plasma.pierce_delay.title: Seconds the torch dwells at the pierce height before dropping to the cut height — long enough to pierce the stock, short enough not to undercut the rim. Typical 0.4 s for 1 mm steel, up to ~1.5 s for 6 mm. Empty = 0.5 s default.
tools.plasma.kerf.title: Plasma cut width (kerf). The toolpath is offset by kerf/2 so the cut edge lands on the geometry — the same compensation a milling cutter gets from its diameter. Measure on your actual stock/amperage. Empty = no kerf compensation (cut on the nominal path).
tools.no_match: No tools match the current filters.
tools.show_all.title: Show every tool in the library, including ones the machine's mode/capabilities can't run. The library is never modified by a mode switch — this only changes the view.
tools.showing_all: Showing all tools — {count} can't run on a {machine} machine
tools.validation_msg: Fix highlighted fields (⌀, RPM, feed, plunge must be > 0).
tools.apply.invalid.title: Fix the highlighted fields before applying.
tools.save.invalid.title: Fix the highlighted fields before saving.
opprops.title: Properties
opprops.empty: Select an operation in the list to edit it.
opprops.name: Name
opprops.tool.edit: Edit this tool in the Tool library
opprops.tool.mismatch.title: This op typically uses {kinds}; running it with the assigned tool will likely produce unexpected gcode.
opprops.tool.title.relief: Ball-nose tool from the project library — its radius drives the scallop + the surface follow.
opprops.tool.title.raster: Laser tool from the project library — its feed rate drives the burn-time estimate.
opprops.tool.title.default: Tool from the project library. Drives the offset radius + feeds + speeds + holder collision check.
opprops.wear.title: This tool has a measured wear offset (Tool library → Wear). Toolpaths are compensated to the effective diameter; the picker above keeps showing the nominal size.
opprops.finish_tool.title.pocket: Optional finish tool. When different from the rough tool, the pipeline runs the bulk cascade with the rough tool, emits a T<n> M6 tool change, then walks the wall ring with this smaller / sharper finish tool at its finish-set feed/speed. Empty = single-tool (the rough tool also defines the wall).
opprops.finish_tool.title.drill: Countersink chamfer cutter. Used only when Chamfer width is set below — after the drill cycle the pipeline emits a toolchange to this V-bit, then walks the hole rim at the chamfer depth. Empty = chamfer with the drill tool itself.
opprops.source.all_chains: runs on every chain in the import
opprops.combine.auto.help: Containment-aware: nested closed objects become holes (outer + inner = annulus). Default.
opprops.combine.union.help: Boolean union of all selected closed contours.
opprops.combine.difference.help: First selected minus the union of the rest.
opprops.combine.intersection.help: Boolean intersection of all selected closed contours.
opprops.combine.xor.help: Symmetric difference (xor) of all selected closed contours.
opprops.combine.none.help: No combination — one boundary per selected object, no holes.
opprops.from_selection.disabled_aria: Select one or more objects in the 2D canvas first to enable this.
opprops.from_selection.title: Use the chains currently highlighted in the 2D pane
opprops.cut.step.reset.title: Clear the override and inherit the tool's default Z step.
opprops.cut.step.required: Step required (set per-op or in the tool library).
opprops.cut.finish_step.title: Optional smaller step for the FINAL Z pass — gives a thin finishing pass at the bottom for cleaner surface. Same sign as Step (negative). Empty = same as Step.
opprops.cut.xy_finish_stock.title: Material left UNCUT on the walls by the roughing pass. A dedicated finish ring walks the actual boundary at the tool's finish-set feed/speed to remove it. Empty / 0 = no allowance (roughing reaches the wall in one pass).
opprops.cut.approach_point.title: Approach point: user-picked XY where the cutter enters each closed ring. Each closed offset's start vertex is rotated to the segment closest to this point — plunge/lead-in then happens there instead of an auto-picked vertex. Empty = auto.
opprops.cut.approach_point.pick.title: Pick the approach point by clicking in the 2D canvas (Shift = disable snap)
opprops.cut.approach_point.picking.title: Picking — click in canvas, ESC to finalize
opprops.cut.approach_point.clear.title: Clear approach point (auto-pick)
opprops.cut.through_depth.title: Cut past the nominal depth by this many mm. Useful for through-cuts on edge-clamped sheet so the cutter clears the bottom. 0 = no extension. Drill ops additionally extend the cut by the tool's tip-cone length automatically (so the full bore diameter reaches the bottom of the stock) — this manual extension stacks on top of the auto-cone.
opprops.cut.depth_list.title: Explicit comma-separated list of Z depths (negative numbers, e.g. -0.5, -1.5, -3). When non-empty, overrides Step / Finish step / Through depth. Empty = use the step-down loop.
opprops.direction.conventional.help: Cutter rotation OPPOSES feed at contact. Safer on machines with backlash; chip starts thin.
opprops.direction.climb.help: Cutter rotation matches feed at contact. Better surface finish; needs a stiff machine.
opprops.plunge.direct.help: Straight Z plunge into material. Safe for center-cutting endmills on shallow steps; risky on harder materials.
opprops.plunge.ramp.help: Ramped descent: cutter walks forward along the path while Z descends, taking a chip in both directions. Required for non-center-cutting bits.
opprops.plunge.helix.help: Helical entry: cutter spirals down on a small circle inside the closed pocket boundary, then walks to the path start. Standard for non-center-cutting endmills and harder materials. Falls back to ramp on open paths or when the helix circle does not fit.
opprops.ramp_angle.title: Ramp angle in degrees. 1°–5° is gentle, 10°+ is aggressive. The ramp's horizontal length is step / tan(angle).
opprops.helix_angle.title: Helix descent angle in degrees. 1°–5° is gentle, 10°+ is aggressive. Each revolution drops Z by 2π·radius·tan(angle).
opprops.helix_autofit.title: Auto-fit the helix circle to the largest inscribed circle inside the pocket boundary. Falls back to ramp when no helix circle fits.
opprops.helix_radius.auto.title: Auto-fit picks the helix radius from the pocket geometry. The detected value previews here before generation; the final fit re-runs at G-code time.
opprops.helix_radius.manual.title: Helix radius in mm. Should be ≥ tool radius; sane default is 1.5 × tool radius. Larger = more clearance, more material removed by the spiral.
opprops.feeds.feed_rate.title: Override the tool's feed rate (mm/min) for this operation only. Leave empty to use the tool default.
opprops.feeds.feed_rate.reset.title: Clear override and inherit from the tool's feed rate.
opprops.feeds.plunge_rate.title: Override the tool's plunge rate (mm/min) for Z descents in this operation. Leave empty to use the tool default.
opprops.feeds.plunge_rate.reset.title: Clear override and inherit from the tool's plunge rate.
opprops.feeds.corner_slow.title: Slow the feed at sharp Line→Line corners by this fraction. 0 = no reduction (default). 0.5 = half feed at corners. Most useful for zigzag pocket fills with their many 180° turns.
opprops.feeds.fraction.title: Unitless fraction between 0 and 1.
opprops.group.label: Label
opprops.group.label.title: Optional phase label. Consecutive ops sharing this value belong to one logical phase (`rough`, `finish`, `drill cycle`, …) and the pipeline emits a `; === GROUP: <name> ===` boundary line at every transition. Empty = no group.
opprops.group.pin_order.title: Pin this operation's position when 'Group ops by tool' is on. A pinned op is a fixed barrier — it keeps its slot and the grouping pass won't move other ops across it. Use to lock a stability-critical cut order (tabs, thin walls).
toolform.form_profile.title: Form / profile cutter cross-section (cove / ogee / dovetail / T-slot / custom). The (z, r) table — height above the tip vs radius — drives the simulator's cut shape. Needs ≥2 rows; otherwise the sim falls back to a tip→diameter taper. Use a preset below or edit rows directly.
toolform.dovetail_dia.title: Widest cutting diameter (at the bottom face) of a dovetail bit.
toolform.angle.title: Flank angle from the tool axis. The radius narrows by tan(angle) per mm of rise. 7°–14° typical.
toolform.cut_height.title: Flute / cutting height — how tall the angled profile is from the bottom face up to the neck.
toolform.generate_dovetail.title: Overwrite the sample table below with a 2-row dovetail profile generated from these inputs.
toolform.tslot_head_dia.title: Widest cutting-disk diameter at the tip of a T-slot / keyway cutter.
toolform.head_thick.title: Height of the cutting disk (how tall the wide undercut head is).
toolform.neck_dia.title: Diameter of the narrow neck above the head — must be smaller than the head ⌀.
toolform.neck_length.title: Length of the narrow neck above the head, up to where the shank begins.
toolform.generate_tslot.title: Overwrite the sample table below with a 4-row T-slot profile (wide disk → narrow neck) generated from these inputs.
toolform.delete_row.title: Delete this sample row
toolform.min_rows_hint: Add at least 2 rows (tip → top) for the sim to carve the real profile.
machinetool.shop_inventory.title: The shop's tool inventory (edit it in the Tool library tab). Greyed-out tools can't run on this machine.
machinetool.filter.title: Filter the inventory by name or kind.
machinetool.inventory_empty: The shop inventory is empty — open the Tool library tab once to seed it from the current tools, then stock machines from here.
machinetool.incompatible_tool.title: A {kind} cannot run on a {machine} machine.
machinetool.already_stocked.title: Already stocked on this machine.
machinetool.add.title: Stock this tool on the machine — it becomes available in operation tool dropdowns.
machinetool.stocked_col.title: Tools loaded on this machine — what operation dropdowns offer. Saved with the machine (profile) and the project.
machinetool.incompatible.title: This tool cannot run on the machine's current mode/capabilities — generate-time warnings will flag operations using it.
machinetool.remove_used.title.one: {n} operation use this tool — reassign them first.
machinetool.remove_used.title.other: {n} operations use this tool — reassign them first.
machinetool.remove_last.title: At least one tool must stay stocked.
machinetool.remove.title: Remove from this machine (the tool stays in the shop inventory).
post.title: Post-processor editor
post.tokens.intro_after: placeholders. Type them verbatim into any template field (header / footer / tool-change). Unknown tokens pass through unchanged.
post.per_axis.hint_before: Rename, reformat, scale, or disable individual axis words. Common uses:
post.per_axis.hint_mid: on Z to flip Z-up, or
post.axes.name: Name
post.axes.scale.title: Linear multiplier applied to the axis value before formatting. -1 flips Z-up vs Z-down; 0.0393701 converts mm to inch. 0 is rejected — it would emit a constant axis word.
post.preview.sub: synthetic 2-pass toolpath: header → tool change → move + plunge + arc + retract → footer · re-renders on every edit
post.import_err.axes_incomplete: JSON has an axes section but one or more axes are missing required fields (enabled / name / format / scale). Skipped axes.
machinews.load_file.title: Replace the active machine settings with a .ivac-machine.json file.
machinews.save_file.title: Save the machine settings to a .ivac-machine.json file (for sharing across computers).
machinews.pick.title: The active machine. Switching applies its settings AND its stocked tools to the project in one undoable step. While a machine is active, edits here are saved back into it.
machinews.new.title: Save the current settings + stocked tools as a new machine and make it active.
machinews.duplicate.title: Copy this machine (settings + tools) under a new name.
machinews.delete.title: Remove this machine from this computer. The project keeps its current settings and tools.
machinews.missing.title: This project references a machine that doesn't exist on this computer. It loaded with its own embedded settings + tools — use New to recreate the machine here.
machinews.tooling.title: Stock this machine from the shop inventory — what operation dropdowns offer.
machinews.settings.title: Machine configuration — speeds, dialect, dimensions, post-processor.
stock.margin.title: Adds to Length + Width (auto-fit case); Thickness is unaffected.
stock.offset_z.title: Z of the stock top plane (mm). 0 = top at the WCS origin (zeroed on the stock top). Positive raises the stock above z=0 — e.g. set it to the stock thickness if you zeroed on the bed. Shifts the 3D stock box, sim heightmap, and out-of-stock check.
stock.work_origin.title: The WCS origin — where on the imported drawing the operator zeroed the machine. Cuts are emitted relative to this point; if it falls outside the geometry bbox the simulator warns. Auto-defaults to the bbox bottom-left on fresh import.
stock.snap_bbox.title: Snap the WCS origin to the bottom-left of the imported drawing's bounding box — the canonical CNC zeroing convention.
playback.scrub.exact: Scrub: the 3D heightfield exactly tracks the playhead — backstep restores the nearest checkpoint and replays only the remainder. Toggle "Exact 3D rewind" off in Settings → Performance for the fastest scrubbing at the price of time-accurate rewind.
playback.scrub.forward: Scrub: the 3D heightfield is forward-only — backstep is a no-op for the sim. Cells retain their deepest-ever cuts. After Generate the playhead sits at 1.0 so the terrain may read as end-of-program until you re-scrub forward. Toggle "Exact 3D rewind" ON in Settings → Performance for time-accurate backstep.
gcode.label: G-code
gcode.stale.title: The project has been edited since this G-code was generated. Click Generate G-code to refresh.
gcode.silenced.title: This op is disabled — commented-out below and hidden in 3D. Toggle the checkbox in the operations list to re-enable. Click Generate to bake the change into the G-code.
about.title: About ivaCAM
about.copy_build_id.title: Copy the build identifier so it can go in a bug report
about.date.title: UTC timestamp the binary was produced at — for cross-timezone bug reports.
about.build_hint: Include the build identifier above when filing issues - it pins the report to the exact binary you tested.
about.third_party.intro: ivaCAM stands on the work of many open-source projects. The major runtime dependencies are listed below; full transitive lists ship in Cargo.lock and package-lock.json.
textlist.collapse.title: Collapse text panel (return to previous panel)
textlist.expand.title: Expand text panel
textlist.title: Text
textlist.add.title: Add text engraving (T)
textlist.empty: No text yet. Click + Add to create an editable text engraving.
textlist.delete.title: Delete text layer (also deletes ops targeting it)
textlist.snap_origin.title: Move origin to the bottom-left of the selection bbox ({x}, {y})
textlist.snap_origin.title_disabled: Select geometry in the canvas first, then snap the text origin to its bottom-left corner
textlist.field.width.title: Horizontal stretch (50–200 %). 100 % = natural font width.
oplist.status.pause: Pause op — emits M0 at this slot. Operator presses Cycle Start to resume.
oplist.status.homing: Homing op — emits G28 at this slot. The controller seeks the home switches; no cutter required.
oplist.status.probe: Probe op — emits a G38.2 probe move at this slot. Touch probe required at the spindle; no cutter.
oplist.status.cycle_marker: Marker op — emits a comment-only `; --- <label> ---` line so pendants / G-code viewers can jump between sections. No motion, no cutter.
oplist.status.gcode_include: G-code include op — splices an external file into the program. The file may carry its own toolchange; no project tool required at this slot.
oplist.status.tool_missing: Tool #{id} is not in the project's tool library. Pick a tool in the operation properties.
oplist.status.no_drawing: No drawing imported yet — open a DXF/SVG to apply this operation.
oplist.status.orphan_objects: Source includes {count} object id(s) not present in the current import — they may have come from a different drawing.
oplist.status.orphan_layers: Source layer(s) "{layers}" not in this drawing.
oplist.status.dirty: Project changed since the last Generate — re-Generate to refresh this operation's G-code.
oplist.status.not_generated: Not generated yet — click Generate to produce this operation's G-code.
oplist.status.up_to_date: Up to date with the last Generate.
oplist.collapse.title: Collapse operations (return to previous panel)
oplist.expand.title: Expand operations panel
oplist.title: Operations
oplist.count.title: Number of operations
oplist.add.title: Add operation
oplist.group_by_tool.title: Reorder operations so same-tool work runs back-to-back, cutting the number of tool changes (a T1/T2/T1 program becomes T1, T1, T2). Matters most on manual machines, where each swap is minutes + a re-probe. Program-only ops and ops with 'Pin order' set stay put. Off = the order shown here.
oplist.empty.title: No operations yet
oplist.empty.sub: An operation tells the machine how to cut a region — pocket, contour, drill, engrave.
oplist.grip.title: Drag to reorder · Alt+Up / Alt+Down with the row focused does the same from the keyboard
oplist.status_btn.title:
{reason}

(click to show in the warnings panel)
oplist.status_btn.aria: Show warnings for {name} in the warnings panel
oplist.repick.title_empty: Source references {objects} object id(s){layers} that no longer exist. Select objects on the canvas, then click Re-pick to attach them to this op.
oplist.repick.title_ready: Replace this op's source with the {count} currently-selected object(s).
oplist.duplicate.title: Duplicate
oplist.delete.title: Delete operation
app.status.pick_approach: Picking approach point — click in canvas to place · Shift = disable snap · ESC = finalize
app.status.tab_placement: Tab placement active — click contour to add a tab · click an existing tab to remove · set Tabs = Off to exit
app.status.hints_selection: Shift = add range · Ctrl/⌘ = toggle · ESC = clear · right-click for context menu
app.status.hints_idle: Click to select · Shift/Ctrl to multi-select · right-click for context menu · ? for shortcuts
app.screen.label: Screen
app.report.tip: Project report — printable job summary (ops, tools, times) to hand to the shop floor
app.stock.name: Stock
app.stock.dims_tip: Stock dimensions (Length × Width × Thickness) in mm
app.stock.dims_tip_current: Current stock dimensions (Length × Width × Thickness) in mm
app.tab.machine_tip: Active machine, its tooling, and its settings
app.tab.settings_tip: App settings — theme, sim safety, performance
app.tab.help_tip: Keyboard & mouse shortcuts and usage help
app.toolbar.open_tip: Open a drawing (DXF / SVG) or a saved .ivac-project.json (Ctrl+O)
app.regions.tip: Show / hide the translucent fill that marks each pocket operation's machined region.
app.regions.label: Regions
app.viewport.threed_tip: Click to switch to 3D. Click again to cycle preview mode: both → wireframe → solid. Shift+click reverses.
app.gcode.toggle_tip: Show / hide the G-code text panel. Click a line to scrub the playhead; the playhead's current line scrolls into view.
app.gcode.label: G-code
app.splitter.gcode_tip: Drag to resize the G-code panel · double-click to reset
app.splitter.sidebar_tip: Drag to resize the side panel · double-click to reset
app.drop.title: Drop to open
shortcuts.title: Keyboard & mouse shortcuts
shortcuts.global.escape: Cancel mode / clear selection / close menu
shortcuts.touch.tap: Select object; tap empty space to deselect
shortcuts.touch.multi_select: Multi-select — add / remove objects without a keyboard
shortcuts.touch.edge_swipe: Switch screens (2D / 3D / Machine / Tools / Settings / Help)
shortcuts.touch.bottom_handles: Drag up to open the G-code and Operations panels
report.title: Project report
report.stats.title: Toolpath stats
report.stats.empty: No G-code generated yet — run Generate first.
report.time.title: Estimated time
report.tools.title: Tools used
report.tools.empty: No tools referenced by enabled operations.
report.tools.col.name: Name
report.ops.title: Operations
report.ops.col.name: Name
report.warnings.title: Warnings
calib.title: Calibrate “{tool}”
calib.step.cut: Cut a short, shallow single-pass slot (one straight line, e.g. an Engrave operation, ~0.5 mm deep) into scrap with this tool, at its normal feed and speed.
calib.step.measure: Measure the width of the slot with a caliper — the slot width is the tool's true cutting diameter.
calib.result: Wear offset: {wear} mm — toolpaths will cut as a {effective} mm tool (nominal {nominal} mm).
calib.implausible: That's more than 25% off the nominal {nominal} mm — double-check the measurement (right slot? mm, not inches?).
calib.invalid: Enter the slot width as a positive number in mm.
calib.current: Current wear offset: {current} mm — applying replaces it.
modeswitch.affected.one: 1 operation uses a tool that cannot run on a {mode} machine.
modeswitch.affected.many: {count} operations use tools that cannot run on a {mode} machine.
modeswitch.none: No tool in the library can run on a {mode} machine.
modeswitch.dismiss.title: Dismiss — nothing is changed. Incompatible assignments are also flagged at Generate time.
phonewarn.title.no_program: No program yet — tap to Generate
phonewarn.title.stale: Toolpath is stale — tap for details / re-Generate
phonewarn.panel.title: Warnings ({count})
phonewarn.empty: No warnings — sim and pipeline are clean.
scene3d.empty.title: No drawing loaded
scene3d.empty.sub: Open a DXF / SVG, drop a file onto the window, or add text.
scene3d.ctx.hint: Click geometry to select objects, then add an operation from them.
